nasa-red.$description: Primary NASA brand red. Red means 'go somewhere.' For primary actions navigating to a new page (.usa-button, .hds-btn--primary) and error/emergency alerts. Never for on-page actions, decorative use, or dataviz.

nasa-red-tint.$description: Lighter tint of NASA red. Used for hover states and error highlights.

nasa-red-shade.$description: Darker shade of NASA red. Used for button hover and error dark.

nasa-blue.$description: Primary NASA brand blue. Blue means 'do something here.' For on-page interactions (.usa-button--secondary, .usa-button--outline), controls, list markers, table headers, info alerts. Never for navigation CTAs or dataviz.

nasa-blue-tint.$description: Lighter tint of NASA blue. Used for active elements and dark palette buttons.

nasa-blue-shade.$description: Darker shade of NASA blue. Used for blue palette background.

international-orange.$description: Secondary brand accent. For status emphasis or decorative markers (blockquote icons). Never for primary actions. Decorative uses exempt from 3:1 contrast minimum.

active-green.$description: Semantic brand green. For active mission status and confirmation states. Never for branding, decorative backgrounds, or dataviz.

carbon-90.$description: Darkest carbon neutral. Default background for dark mode.

carbon-black.$description: Pure black for maximum contrast text and outlines.

carbon-05.$description: Lightest carbon neutral. Alternate background for light mode.

spacesuit-white.$description: Pure white for maximum contrast text and default light backgrounds.

color.$description: Base color primitives for NASA-branded *.nasa.gov UIs. Foundation for themed palettes and non-data chart elements (labels, gridlines, annotations). For data-encoding colors (series, fills, scales), use dataviz domain tokens instead.

red.$description: Core sequential hue. For warm temperatures, negative values, or intensity. Diverging pair: Red + Blue (meet at white center). Never mix hues within one sequential scale.

orange.$description: Core sequential hue. For continuous data where blue, red, or green carry unwanted semantic associations. No predefined diverging pair. Never mix hues within one sequential scale.

yellow.$description: Additional sequential hue. No predefined diverging pair. Use to supplement core hues when additional scales are needed. Never use as primary default.

gold.$description: Additional sequential hue. No predefined diverging pair. Use to supplement core hues when additional scales are needed. Never use as primary default.

lime.$description: Additional sequential hue. No predefined diverging pair. Use to supplement core hues when additional scales are needed. Never use as primary default.

green.$description: Core sequential hue. For positive values or growth metrics. Diverging pair: Green + Purple (neutral, no warm/cool connotation). Never mix hues within one sequential scale.

aqua.$description: Additional sequential hue. Diverging pair: Aqua + Magenta (high perceptual contrast). Use when core hues are exhausted or carry conflicting semantics. Never use as primary default scale.

slate.$description: Additional sequential hue. No predefined diverging pair. Use to supplement core hues when additional scales are needed. Never use as primary default.

blue.$description: Core sequential hue. Default for cool temperatures, above/below average, or general continuous data. Diverging pair: Blue + Red (meet at white center). Never mix hues within one sequential scale.

purple.$description: Additional sequential hue. Diverging pair: Purple + Green (neutral). Use when core hues are exhausted or carry conflicting semantics. Never use as primary default scale.

magenta.$description: Additional sequential hue. Diverging pair: Magenta + Aqua (high perceptual contrast). Use when core hues are exhausted or carry conflicting semantics. Never use as primary default scale.

seq.$description: Single-hue 10-step scales on OKLCh curves for continuous, ordered data (heatmaps, choropleths, density). Never use for unrelated categories. For continuous scales, interpolate in OKLCh/OKLab space. On dark backgrounds, invert so lighter = higher.

light.$description: Categorical palette for light backgrounds (white, light, midtone, blue themes). 12 distinct values. Never use on dark backgrounds.

dark.$description: Categorical palette for dark backgrounds (dark, black themes). 12 distinct values. Never use on light backgrounds.

set.$description: Predefined optimized subsets of categorical colors for 1-5 category charts. 4 options per group size, chosen for maximum perceptual contrast. For 6+ categories, use individual cat tokens in numeric order instead.

b.$description: Set assignment: cat.light.3 (Orange ~38, optimized for categorical contrast)
Set assignment: cat.light.7 (Red ~79, optimized for categorical contrast)

a.$description: Set assignment: cat.light.3 (Orange ~38, optimized for categorical contrast)

2-items.$description: 2-category optimized subsets. 4 options maximizing perceptual contrast. Use any single option — do not mix across options.

b.$description: Set assignment: cat.light.3 (Orange ~38, optimized for categorical contrast)
Set assignment: cat.light.7 (Red ~79, optimized for categorical contrast)

a.$description: Set assignment: cat.light.3 (Orange ~38, optimized for categorical contrast)

3-items.$description: 3-category optimized subsets. 4 options maximizing perceptual contrast. Use any single option — do not mix across options.

b.$description: Set assignment: cat.light.3 (Orange ~38, optimized for categorical contrast)
Set assignment: cat.light.7 (Red ~79, optimized for categorical contrast)

a.$description: Set assignment: cat.light.3 (Orange ~38, optimized for categorical contrast)

4-items.$description: 4-category optimized subsets. 4 options maximizing perceptual contrast. Use any single option — do not mix across options.

b.$description: Set assignment: cat.light.3 (Orange ~38, optimized for categorical contrast)

e.$description: Set assignment: cat.light.5 (Gold ~38, optimized for categorical contrast)

b.$description: Set assignment: cat.light.7 (Red ~79, optimized for categorical contrast)

a.$description: Set assignment: cat.light.3 (Orange ~38, optimized for categorical contrast)

e.$description: Set assignment: cat.light.7 (Red ~79, optimized for categorical contrast)
Set assignment: cat.light.7 (Red ~79, optimized for categorical contrast)

5-items.$description: 5-category optimized subsets. 4 options maximizing perceptual contrast. Use any single option — do not mix across options.

2-items.$description: 2-category optimized subsets. 4 options maximizing perceptual contrast. Use any single option — do not mix across options.

3-items.$description: 3-category optimized subsets. 4 options maximizing perceptual contrast. Use any single option — do not mix across options.

4-items.$description: 4-category optimized subsets. 4 options maximizing perceptual contrast. Use any single option — do not mix across options.

5-items.$description: 5-category optimized subsets. 4 options maximizing perceptual contrast. Use any single option — do not mix across options.

cat.$description: Distinct-hue palettes for comparing unrelated categories in charts. For 1-5 categories, use a predefined set option from cat.set. For 6+, use colors in numeric order starting from 1.

color.$description: Dataviz color scales for chart data series. Never for non-data UI. Add 1px divider between contiguous fills. To highlight: use saturated dark hue (e.g., blue-70) for focus, desaturated light hue (e.g., slate-40) for baseline. Contrast both hue and value.

dataviz.$description: Data visualization design tokens for NASA-branded *.nasa.gov sites. Only for charts, graphs, and data displays. Never apply to general UI elements or page layout.

thin.$description: Role token for single-pixel borders: card, inputs, table.

thick.$description: Role token for emphasis borders: button outlines.

radius.$description: HDS uses binary corner treatment: 0 for UI controls (buttons, inputs, cards), 50% for circles. No intermediate radii.

focus.$description: Dashed focus ring for keyboard navigation. Palette-aware via --hds-palette-focus-* custom properties. Used by buttons, links, pagination, icon buttons, accordions, breadcrumbs, table sort, and the global baseline. Solid focus states on form controls (text input border, checkbox/radio inner box) are not tokenized at this time.

width.$description: Dashed stroke width. Standardized across all dashed focus rings.

style.$description: All dashed focus rings use a 2,3 dasharray.

offset.$description: Visual gap between element and ring. May be achieved differently per mechanic (negative inset, outline-offset, etc.) but the visual result is consistent.

max-width.$description: Maximum content width. Aligns with desktop-lg breakpoint.

sm.$description: Used on mobile and mobile-lg breakpoints.

md.$description: Used on tablet through desktop-lg breakpoints.

xl.$description: Grid gutter at TV breakpoint. Not yet implemented in SCSS.

0.$description: Zero spacing. Use to explicitly collapse gaps.

1.$description: Tightest content relationship. Use for closely related elements within a single component.

2.$description: Element stacking. Default for horizontal and vertical spacing between sibling elements.

3.$description: Content section separation within the same component area.

4.$description: Component separation at mobile and tablet breakpoints.

8.$description: Section-level vertical padding for major layout blocks (.usa-section).

9.$description: Component separation at widescreen and TV breakpoints.

15.$description: Layout section division at desktop-lg and below.

30.$description: Layout section division at TV breakpoint.

spacing.$description: HDS spacing scale. Primary values (whole numbers) are the recommended design increments for layout and component separation. Intermediate values (fractional) are for component-internal spacing only.

0.5.$description: Intermediate. Compact table cell padding, sub-link gaps in in-page nav. Not for layout spacing.

1.5.$description: Intermediate. Table cell padding, button block padding, nav indentation, list marker gaps. Most common component-internal value.

2.5.$description: Intermediate. List item separation, button inline padding, form label margins, blockquote attribution spacing.

breakpoint.$description: HDS responsive breakpoints. These override USWDS defaults for NASA site layouts.

desktop-lg.$description: Large desktop. Container max-width aligns here.

widescreen.$description: Widescreen. Grid gutter increases to 24px. Not yet implemented in SCSS.

tv.$description: TV/large display. Grid gutter increases to 32px. Not yet implemented in SCSS.
